qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype Pollution via merge Functional API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding claims vulnerability in functional merge() API with code references to src/functional/merge.js, but the advisory describes prototype pollution in mergeDeep(), merge(), Map.toJS(), and Map.toObject() APIs. The PoC execution with immutable@5.1.4 and 4.3.6 failed to demonstrate prototype pollution, and the advisory mentions the vulnerable versions are patched in 3.8.3, 4.3.7, and 5.1.5. The finding's code locations do not clearly match the advisory's description of the vulnerable APIs, and runtime evidence contradicts the vulnerability claim.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype Pollution via setIn Functional API
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding claims prototype pollution via setIn() functional API, but the advisory does not mention setIn() as an affected API. The advisory specifically lists mergeDeep(), mergeDeepWith(), merge(), Map.toJS(), and Map.toObject() as the vulnerable APIs. PoC execution failed to demonstrate the vulnerability with immutable@5.1.4 and 4.3.6. This finding describes a different attack vector than what the advisory describes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Hash Collision Attack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes hash collision DoS vulnerability, which is unrelated to the advisory's prototype pollution vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-wf6x-7x77-mvgw) is specifically about prototype pollution in merge, mergeDeep, Map.toJS, and Map.toObject APIs. This finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Stack Overflow in fromJS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes stack overflow DoS in fromJS(), which is unrelated to the advisory's prototype pollution vulnerability. The advisory is about prototype pollution in merge, mergeDeep, Map.toJS, and Map.toObject APIs, not about stack overflow in fromJS. This finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via valueOf During Object Hashing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes arbitrary code execution via valueOf() during object hashing, which is unrelated to the advisory's prototype pollution vulnerability. The advisory is about prototype pollution via merge/mergeDeep/Map.toJS/Map.toObject APIs, not about valueOf code injection. This finding does not match the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding successfully identifies and demonstrates the advisory-described prototype pollution vulnerability in immutable. Two findings claim prototype pollution in functional merge() and setIn() APIs, but PoC execution with both immutable@5.1.4 and 4.3.6 failed to demonstrate the vulnerability. The findings reference code locations (src/functional/merge.js, src/utils/shallowCopy.ts) that cannot be verified without source code access. The other findings describe unrelated vulnerabilities (hash collision DoS, stack overflow DoS, valueOf injection, deserialization RCE) that do not match the advisory's description of prototype pollution in merge, mergeDeep, Map.toJS, and Map.toObject APIs.
evidence: Advisory describes prototype pollution in merge, mergeDeep, Map.toJS, Map.toObject APIs
Finding 'prototype-pollution-merge' claims vulnerability in functional merge() with PoC that failed to pollute prototype when tested with immutable@5.1.4 and 4.3.6
Finding 'prototype-pollution-setin' claims vulnerability in setIn() which is not listed as an affected API in the advisory
PoC execution logs show Object.prototype.polluted remains undefined after merge and setIn operations
Other findings describe unrelated vulnerabilities: hash collision DoS, stack overflow in fromJS, valueOf code injection, and deserialization RCE in an application endpoint
